meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelance SaaS Workflow: Scaling Without Deep Code Knowledge
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between client requirements and SaaS delivery. Master this high-speed workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code for professional results.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of deep SaaS knowledge, implement a standardized, AI-assisted delivery pipeline. Use Antigravity to architect your schema and Claude Code to execute the boilerplate. This approach transforms complex backend requirements into manageable, modular components, allowing you to ship professional-grade SaaS applications at speed without sacrificing quality.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, your client’s deadline is looming, and you’re staring at a blank terminal, paralyzed by the sheer complexity of the SaaS architecture they’ve requested. You have the vision and the design eye, but the technical debt of 'not knowing' is threatening to derail your reputation. This is the bottleneck that kills freelance careers.

But what if you didn't have to be a backend engineer to build like one? By shifting your workflow from manual coding to intelligent orchestration using Antigravity and Claude Code, you can turn abstract requirements into functional, scalable SaaS modules in hours rather than weeks. This isn't just about building; it's about building with the precision of a veteran architect.

steps.title: Architecting the Data Layer
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, define your schema clearly. Use the AI to generate a robust database architecture that handles multi-tenancy from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior SaaS Architect. Define a multi-tenant PostgreSQL schema for a [Project Type] application including users, organizations, and subscription tiers. Ensure strictly enforced foreign key constraints and indexed lookups.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate the Prisma schema based on the architecture defined in the Antigravity output.'

steps.title: Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Standardize your project structure to ensure maintainability. Use Claude Code to scaffold the initial repository with industry-standard patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a directory structure for a Next.js 14 SaaS application using App Router, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI. Include folders for services, hooks, and components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize a new Next.js project with the specified structure and install required dependencies.'

steps.title: Implementing Authentication
steps.description: Authentication is the highest risk area. Use proven patterns to avoid security vulnerabilities while keeping the implementation fast.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a secure authentication flow using Auth.js (NextAuth) with GitHub and Email providers, ensuring session data is synced with our tenant ID from the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the auth.ts configuration and wrap the root layout in the AuthProvider.'

steps.title: Automating API Routes
steps.description: Don't write CRUD endpoints manually. Use AI to generate type-safe API routes that interact with your schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standardized API handler for [Resource] that includes validation via Zod, error handling, and authorization checks based on tenant access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate the route.ts file for the [Resource] endpoint using the Zod validation schema provided.'

steps.title: Final Quality Assurance
steps.description: Run automated checks to ensure the code meets professional standards and is ready for production deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for common security flaws, performance bottlenecks, and adherence to clean code principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Run a security audit on the current project and suggest refactors for any detected vulnerabilities.'

pro_tips: Always force the AI to use Zod schemas for validation; it prevents 90% of runtime errors in SaaS apps.
Use a 'Documentation-First' approach: have Claude Code generate a README.md before coding so the context is preserved for future client handovers.
Inject your 'SaaS DNA' into the system prompt: tell the AI your preferred stack, naming conventions, and folder structure once, and save it as a custom profile.
Treat your database schema as the 'Source of Truth'—if the schema is wrong, the app is wrong. Validate the schema with an AI peer review before coding.
When a client asks for a feature, don't write code immediately. Use Antigravity to map the feature to existing modules to avoid bloat.
content.next_steps: Once the core SaaS structure is deployed, focus on building the 'Golden Path'—the single workflow that provides the most value to your client—and iterate based on user feedback.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with the technical unknown and start building like a senior developer. Our community provides the templates, prompts, and peer reviews you need to scale your freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
